items.headline: Eastern Conference Finals Locked: Cavs vs. Knicks!
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in a thrilling Game 7 on Sunday, May 17, advancing to face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals. The Knicks hold homecourt advantage for the series.

items.headline: Western Conference Finals Tip-Off Tonight: Thunder Host Spurs!
items.summary: The Western Conference Finals are set to begin today, May 18, with the Oklahoma City Thunder hosting the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1. The Thunder swept the Los Angeles Lakers, while the Spurs dispatched the Minnesota Timberwolves in six games.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Repeats as NBA MVP!
items.summary: Oklahoma City Thunder superstar Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been named the NBA's Most Valuable Player for the second consecutive year on Sunday, May 17. He became the 18th player to win at least two MVP awards and the 14th to win them in back-to-back fashion.

items.headline: Blockbuster Trade Rumors Swirl: Giannis, LeBron, Morant in Play?
items.summary: The NBA offseason is buzzing with major trade and free agency speculation. The Milwaukee Bucks are reportedly 'open for business' on trade offers for two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo. LeBron James is weighing a potential return to Cleveland or a move to Golden State. Additionally, Ja Morant's future with the Grizzlies is uncertain, drawing interest from multiple teams, including the Timberwolves.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Shines in Playoffs for Trail Blazers Despite Exit
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija delivered impressive performances for the Portland Trail Blazers in their playoff run, including a 41-point, 12-assist, 7-rebound, 2-block effort in a Play-In victory over the Phoenix Suns. He also recorded 19 points, 9 assists, 6 rebounds and 22 points, 3 assists, 3 rebounds in games against the San Antonio Spurs in the first round. The Blazers ultimately fell to the Spurs in five games.

items.headline: Cavaliers Dominate Pistons in Game 7 to Advance!
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers secured their spot in the Eastern Conference Finals with a decisive Game 7 victory over the Detroit Pistons.

items.statContext: Led Cavaliers in Game 7 win over Pistons.
Key scoring contributions in Cavaliers' Game 7 victory.
Named NBA MVP for the second consecutive season.
Strong scoring in Spurs' Game 6 win vs. Timberwolves.
Masterclass in Play-In win against the Suns.

items.headline: Thunder and Spurs Open Western Conference Finals Tonight
items.summary: Get ready for Western Conference Finals action as the OKC Thunder host the San Antonio Spurs for Game 1 tonight, Monday, May 18, at 8:30 PM ET.

items.headline: Key Injury Updates Ahead of Conference Finals
items.summary: The Thunder's Jalen Williams is questionable for Game 1 of the WCF due to a hamstring injury. For the Spurs, Luke Kornet and De'Aaron Fox are also listed as questionable. Good news for the Knicks, as OG Anunoby is expected to be ready for the start of the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.headline: Lakers' Austin Reaves Expected to Test Free Agency
items.summary: Austin Reaves is widely expected to opt out of his player option for 2026-27 to become an unrestricted free agent. The Chicago Bulls and Brooklyn Nets are reportedly among teams showing interest.

items.headline: Western Conference Finals Game 1
items.summary: The highly anticipated Western Conference Finals kick off tonight in Oklahoma City.
